title: ferray-core: no __array_function__-style dispatch protocol for duck-typing
description:
Question: 

Context: 

Findings: 

NumPy's __array_function__ and __array_ufunc__ protocols allow third-party array types (Dask, CuPy, JAX) to intercept NumPy function calls and provide their own implementations. This is how the NumPy ecosystem achieves backend-agnostic code. ferray has no equivalent dispatch mechanism. In Rust this could be modeled as a trait (ArrayLike) that ferray functions are generic over, allowing downstream crates to plug in GPU arrays, distributed arrays, or lazy arrays. This is a long-term architectural consideration — research what a Rust-native dispatch protocol would look like.
